ddls:
CREATE TABLE "ordinances" (
  "ordinance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ordinance */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the ordinance is in effect */,
  "county" TEXT /* County where the ordinance is in effect */,
  "citation" TEXT /* Citation of the ordinance */,
  "language" TEXT /* Full text of the ordinance */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for further inquiries */,
  "date_enacted" TEXT /* Date the ordinance was enacted or last updated */,
  "ordinance_type" TEXT /* Type of ordinance (e.g., beekeeping, zoning, agricultural) */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Brief summary of the ordinance */,
  "amended_date" TEXT /* Date when the ordinance was last amended */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the ordinance becomes effective */,
  "repealed_date" TEXT /* Date when the ordinance was repealed (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ordinance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ordinances_city FOREIGN KEY ("city") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ordinances_county FOREIGN KEY ("county") REFERENCES counties ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the county in which the city is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the city in square miles or square kilometers */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "county_name" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the county */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the county in square miles or square kilometers */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the county */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the county */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "beekeepers" (
  "beekeeper_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each beekeeper */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the beekeeper */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the beekeeper */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city where the beekeeper operates */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the beekeeper */,
  "experience_years" INTEGER /* Number of years of experience in beekeeping */,
  "registered_hives" INTEGER /* Number of registered hives */,
  "last_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the last compliance inspection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("beekeeper_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_beekeepers_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "beekeeper_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the beekeeper who submitted the application */,
  "ordinance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the ordinance the application pertains to */,
  "submission_date" TEXT /* Date the application was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., pending, approved, denied) */,
  "application_type" TEXT /* Type of application (e.g., new, renewal) */,
  "hives_count" INTEGER /* Number of hives the application pertains to */,
  "inspection_required" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if an inspection is required */,
  "inspection_date" TEXT /* Date when the inspection is scheduled */,
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the inspector assigned to the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_ordinance_id FOREIGN KEY ("ordinance_id") REFERENCES ordinances ("ordinance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_inspector_id FOREIGN KEY ("inspector_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_beekeeper_id FOREIGN KEY ("beekeeper_id") REFERENCES beekeepers ("beekeeper_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance" (
  "compliance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance event */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application associated with the compliance event */,
  "inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the compliance inspection */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the compliance inspection */,
  "inspector_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the inspector who conducted the inspection */,
  "issues_identified" TEXT /* Text describing any issues identified during the inspection */,
  "followup_required" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if follow-up actions are required */,
  "followup_date" TEXT /* Date when follow-up actions are due */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compliance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_inspector_id FOREIGN KEY ("inspector_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, city planner, public) */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city associated with the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "ordinance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the ordinance accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the ordinance */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the ordinance was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser used to access the platform */,
  "duration_seconds" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_ordinance_id FOREIGN KEY ("ordinance_id") REFERENCES ordinances ("ordinance_id")
)
